package org.apache.fluo.core.oracle;

import java.io.IOException;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.concurrent.ArrayBlockingQueue;
import java.util.concurrent.Callable;
import java.util.concurrent.CountDownLatch;
import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;
import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;

import com.codahale.metrics.Histogram;
import com.codahale.metrics.Timer;
import com.codahale.metrics.Timer.Context;
import com.google.common.util.concurrent.ListenableFuture;
import com.google.common.util.concurrent.ListenableFutureTask;
import org.apache.curator.framework.CuratorFramework;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.cache.PathChildrenCache;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.cache.PathChildrenCacheEvent;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.cache.PathChildrenCacheListener;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.leader.LeaderSelector;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.leader.LeaderSelectorListenerAdapter;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.leader.Participant;
import org.apache.fluo.accumulo.util.ZookeeperPath;
import org.apache.fluo.api.exceptions.FluoException;
import org.apache.fluo.core.impl.CuratorCnxnListener;
import org.apache.fluo.core.impl.Environment;
import org.apache.fluo.core.metrics.MetricsUtil;
import org.apache.fluo.core.thrift.OracleService;
import org.apache.fluo.core.thrift.Stamps;
import org.apache.fluo.core.util.CuratorUtil;
import org.apache.fluo.core.util.UtilWaitThread;
import org.apache.thrift.TException;
import org.apache.thrift.protocol.TCompactProtocol;
import org.apache.thrift.protocol.TProtocol;
import org.apache.thrift.transport.TFastFramedTransport;
import org.apache.thrift.transport.TSocket;
import org.apache.thrift.transport.TTransport;
import org.apache.thrift.transport.TTransportException;
import org.apache.zookeeper.KeeperException;
import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;

/**
 * Connects to an oracle to retrieve timestamps. If multiple oracle servers are run, it will
 * automatically fail over to different leaders.
 */
public class OracleClient implements AutoCloseable {

  public static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(OracleClient.class);
  private static final int MAX_ORACLE_WAIT_PERIOD = 60;

  private final Timer responseTimer;
  private final Histogram stampsHistogram;

  private Participant currentLeader;

  private static final class TimeRequest implements Callable<Stamp> {
    CountDownLatch cdl = new CountDownLatch(1);
    AtomicReference<Stamp> stampRef = new AtomicReference<>();
    ListenableFutureTask<Stamp> lf = null;

    @Override
    public Stamp call() throws Exception {
      return stampRef.get();
    }
  }

  private class TimestampRetriever extends LeaderSelectorListenerAdapter implements Runnable,
      PathChildrenCacheListener {

    private LeaderSelector leaderSelector;
    private CuratorFramework curatorFramework;
    private OracleService.Client client;
    private PathChildrenCache pathChildrenCache;

    private TTransport transport;

    @Override
    public void run() {

      try {
        synchronized (this) {
          // want this code to be mutually exclusive with close() .. so if in middle of setup, close
          // method will wait till finished
          if (closed.get()) {
            return;
          }

          curatorFramework = CuratorUtil.newAppCurator(env.getConfiguration());
          CuratorCnxnListener cnxnListener = new CuratorCnxnListener();
          curatorFramework.getConnectionStateListenable().addListener(cnxnListener);
          curatorFramework.start();

          while (!cnxnListener.isConnected()) {
            Thread.sleep(200);
          }

          leaderSelector = new LeaderSelector(curatorFramework, ZookeeperPath.ORACLE_SERVER, this);

          pathChildrenCache =
              new PathChildrenCache(curatorFramework, ZookeeperPath.ORACLE_SERVER, true);
          pathChildrenCache.getListenable().addListener(this);
          pathChildrenCache.start();

          connect();
        }
        doWork();
      } catch (Exception e) {
        if (!closed.get()) {
          log.error("Exception occurred in run() method", e);
        } else {
          log.debug("Exception occurred in run() method", e);
        }
      }
    }

    /**
     * It's possible an Oracle has gone into a bad state. Upon the leader being changed, we want to
     * update our state
     */
    @Override
    public void childEvent(CuratorFramework curatorFramework, PathChildrenCacheEvent event)
        throws Exception {

      if (event.getType().equals(PathChildrenCacheEvent.Type.CHILD_REMOVED)
          || event.getType().equals(PathChildrenCacheEvent.Type.CHILD_ADDED)
          || event.getType().equals(PathChildrenCacheEvent.Type.CHILD_UPDATED)) {

        Participant participant = leaderSelector.getLeader();
        synchronized (this) {
          if (isLeader(participant)) {
            currentLeader = leaderSelector.getLeader();
          } else {
            currentLeader = null;
          }
        }
      }
    }

    private void doWork() {

      ArrayList<TimeRequest> request = new ArrayList<>();

      while (true) {

        try {
          request.clear();
          TimeRequest trh = null;
          while (trh == null) {
            if (closed.get()) {
              return;
            }
            trh = queue.poll(1, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
          }
          request.add(trh);
          queue.drainTo(request);

          long txStampsStart;
          long gcStamp;

          while (true) {

            try {
              String currentLeaderId;
              OracleService.Client localClient;
              synchronized (this) {
                currentLeaderId = getOracle();
                localClient = client;
              }

              final Context timerContext = responseTimer.time();

              Stamps stamps = localClient.getTimestamps(env.getFluoApplicationID(), request.size());
              txStampsStart = stamps.txStampsStart;
              gcStamp = stamps.gcStamp;

              String leaderId = getOracle();
              if (leaderId != null && !leaderId.equals(currentLeaderId)) {
                reconnect();
                continue;
              }

              stampsHistogram.update(request.size());
              timerContext.close();

              break;

            } catch (TTransportException tte) {
              log.info("Oracle connection lost. Retrying...");
              reconnect();
            } catch (TException e) {
              log.error("TException occurred in doWork() method", e);
            }
          }

          for (int i = 0; i < request.size(); i++) {
            TimeRequest tr = request.get(i);
            tr.stampRef.set(new Stamp(txStampsStart + i, gcStamp));
            if (tr.lf == null) {
              tr.cdl.countDown();
            } else {
              tr.lf.run();
            }
          }
        } catch (InterruptedException e) {
          if (!closed.get()) {
            log.error("InterruptedException occurred in doWork() method", e);
          } else {
            log.debug("InterruptedException occurred in doWork() method", e);
          }
        } catch (Exception e) {
          log.error("Exception occurred in doWork() method", e);
        }
      }
    }

    private synchronized void connect() throws IOException, KeeperException, InterruptedException,
        TTransportException {

      getLeader();
      while (true) {
        log.debug("Connecting to oracle at " + currentLeader.getId());
        String[] hostAndPort = currentLeader.getId().split(":");

        String host = hostAndPort[0];
        int port = Integer.parseInt(hostAndPort[1]);

        try {
          transport = new TFastFramedTransport(new TSocket(host, port));
          transport.open();
          TProtocol protocol = new TCompactProtocol(transport);
          client = new OracleService.Client(protocol);
          log.info("Connected to oracle at " + getOracle());
          break;
        } catch (TTransportException e) {
          sleepRandom();
          getLeader();
        } catch (Exception e) {
          throw new RuntimeException(e);
        }
      }
    }

    /**
     * Atomically closes current connection and connects to the current leader
     */
    private synchronized void reconnect() throws InterruptedException, TTransportException,
        KeeperException, IOException {
      if (transport.isOpen()) {
        transport.close();
      }
      connect();
    }

    private synchronized void close() {
      if (transport != null && transport.isOpen()) {
        transport.close();
      }
      try {
        if (pathChildrenCache != null) {
          pathChildrenCache.close();
        }
      } catch (IOException e) {
        throw new RuntimeException(e);
      }

      if (curatorFramework != null) {
        curatorFramework.close();
      }

      transport = null;
      pathChildrenCache = null;
      leaderSelector = null;
      curatorFramework = null;
    }

    private boolean getLeaderAttempt() {
      Participant possibleLeader = null;
      try {
        possibleLeader = leaderSelector.getLeader();
      } catch (KeeperException e) {
        log.debug("Exception throw in getLeaderAttempt()", e);
      } catch (Exception e) {
        throw new RuntimeException(e);
      }

      if (isLeader(possibleLeader)) {
        currentLeader = possibleLeader;
        return true;
      }
      return false;
    }

    /**
     * Attempt to retrieve a leader until one is found
     */
    private void getLeader() {
      boolean found = getLeaderAttempt();
      while (!found) {
        sleepRandom();
        found = getLeaderAttempt();
      }
    }

    /**
     * Sleep a random amount of time from 100ms to 1sec
     */
    private void sleepRandom() {
      UtilWaitThread.sleep(100 + (long) (1000 * Math.random()), closed);
    }

    private boolean isLeader(Participant participant) {
      return participant != null && participant.isLeader();
    }

    /**
     * NOTE: This isn't competing for leadership, so it doesn't need to be started.
     */
    @Override
    public void takeLeadership(CuratorFramework curatorFramework) throws Exception {}
  }

  private final Environment env;
  private final ArrayBlockingQueue<TimeRequest> queue = new ArrayBlockingQueue<>(10000);
  private final Thread thread;
  private AtomicBoolean closed = new AtomicBoolean(false);
  private final TimestampRetriever timestampRetriever;

  public OracleClient(Environment env) {
    this.env = env;
    responseTimer =
        MetricsUtil.getTimer(env.getConfiguration(), env.getSharedResources().getMetricRegistry(),
            env.getMetricNames().getOracleResponseTime());
    stampsHistogram =
        MetricsUtil.getHistogram(env.getConfiguration(), env.getSharedResources()
            .getMetricRegistry(), env.getMetricNames().getOracleClientStamps());
    timestampRetriever = new TimestampRetriever();
    thread = new Thread(timestampRetriever);
    thread.setDaemon(true);
    thread.start();
  }

  /**
   * Retrieves time stamp from Oracle. Throws {@link FluoException} if timed out or interrupted.
   */
  public Stamp getStamp() {
    checkClosed();

    TimeRequest tr = new TimeRequest();
    try {
      queue.put(tr);
      int timeout = env.getConfiguration().getClientRetryTimeout();
      if (timeout < 0) {
        long waitPeriod = 1;
        long waitTotal = 0;
        while (!tr.cdl.await(waitPeriod, TimeUnit.SECONDS)) {
          checkClosed();
          waitTotal += waitPeriod;
          if (waitPeriod < MAX_ORACLE_WAIT_PERIOD) {
            waitPeriod *= 2;
          }
          log.warn(
              "Waiting for timestamp from Oracle. Is it running? waitTotal={}s waitPeriod={}s",
              waitTotal, waitPeriod);
        }
      } else if (!tr.cdl.await(timeout, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)) {
        throw new FluoException("Timed out (after " + timeout
            + "ms) trying to retrieve timestamp from Oracle.  Is the Oracle running?");
      }
    } catch (InterruptedException e) {
      throw new FluoException("Interrupted while retrieving timestamp from Oracle", e);
    }
    return tr.stampRef.get();
  }

  public ListenableFuture<Stamp> getStampAsync() {
    checkClosed();

    TimeRequest tr = new TimeRequest();
    ListenableFutureTask<Stamp> lf = ListenableFutureTask.create(tr);
    tr.lf = lf;
    try {
      queue.put(tr);
    } catch (InterruptedException e) {
      throw new RuntimeException(e);
    }
    return lf;
  }

  /**
   * Return the oracle that the current client is connected to.
   */
  public synchronized String getOracle() {
    checkClosed();
    return currentLeader != null ? currentLeader.getId() : null;
  }

  private void checkClosed() {
    if (closed.get()) {
      throw new IllegalStateException(OracleClient.class.getSimpleName() + " is closed");
    }
  }

  @Override
  public void close() {
    if (!closed.get()) {
      closed.set(true);
      try {
        thread.interrupt();
        thread.join();
        timestampRetriever.close();
      } catch (InterruptedException e) {
        throw new FluoException("Interrupted during close", e);
      }
    }
  }
}

package org.apache.fluo.core.worker;

import java.io.File;
import java.util.Objects;

import com.google.common.base.Preconditions;
import org.apache.curator.framework.recipes.cache.NodeCache;
import org.apache.fluo.api.config.FluoConfiguration;
import org.apache.fluo.api.exceptions.FluoException;
import org.apache.fluo.api.service.FluoWorker;
import org.apache.fluo.core.impl.Environment;
import org.apache.fluo.core.metrics.ReporterUtil;
import org.apache.fluo.core.util.CuratorUtil;
import org.apache.fluo.core.util.UtilWaitThread;
import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;

public class FluoWorkerImpl implements FluoWorker {

  private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(FluoWorkerImpl.class);

  private FluoConfiguration config;
  private Environment env;
  private AutoCloseable reporters;
  private NotificationProcessor np;
  private NotificationFinder notificationFinder;
  private NodeCache appIdCache;

  public FluoWorkerImpl(FluoConfiguration config) {
    Objects.requireNonNull(config);
    Preconditions.checkArgument(config.hasRequiredWorkerProps());
    this.config = config;
  }

  @Override
  public void start() {
    try {
      env = new Environment(config);
      reporters = ReporterUtil.setupReporters(env);
      appIdCache = CuratorUtil.startAppIdWatcher(env);

      log.info("Starting Worker for Fluo '{}' application with the following configuration:",
          config.getApplicationName());
      env.getConfiguration().print();

      np = new NotificationProcessor(env);
      notificationFinder = NotificationFinderFactory.newNotificationFinder(env.getConfiguration());
      notificationFinder.init(env, np);
      notificationFinder.start();
    } catch (Exception e) {
      throw new FluoException(e);
    }
  }

  @Override
  public void stop() {
    try {
      notificationFinder.stop();
      np.close();
      appIdCache.close();
      reporters.close();
      env.close();
    } catch (Exception e) {
      throw new FluoException(e);
    }
  }

  public static void main(String[] args) {
    if (args.length != 1) {
      System.err.println("Usage: FluoWorkerImpl <fluoPropsPath>");
      System.exit(-1);
    }
    String propsPath = args[0];
    Objects.requireNonNull(propsPath);
    File propsFile = new File(propsPath);
    if (!propsFile.exists()) {
      System.err.println("ERROR - Fluo properties file does not exist: " + propsPath);
      System.exit(-1);
    }
    Preconditions.checkArgument(propsFile.exists());
    try {
      FluoConfiguration config = new FluoConfiguration(propsFile);
      FluoWorkerImpl worker = new FluoWorkerImpl(config);
      worker.start();
      while (true) {
        UtilWaitThread.sleep(10000);
      }
    } catch (Exception e) {
      log.error("Exception running FluoWorker: ", e);
    }
  }
}

# Sets HADOOP_PREFIX if it is not already set.  Please modify the
# export statement to use the correct directory.  Remove the test
# statement to override any previously set environment.

test -z "$HADOOP_PREFIX" && export HADOOP_PREFIX=/path/to/hadoop

# The classpath for Fluo must be defined.  The Fluo tarball does not include
# jars for Accumulo, Zookeeper, or Hadoop.  This example env file offers two
# ways to setup the classpath with these jars.  Go to the end of the file for
# more info.

addToClasspath() 
{
  local dir=$1
  local filterRegex=$2

  if [ ! -d "$dir" ]; then
    echo "ERROR $dir does not exist or not a directory"
    exit 1
  fi

  for jar in $dir/*.jar; do
    if ! [[ $jar =~ $filterRegex ]]; then
       CLASSPATH="$CLASSPATH:$jar"
    fi
  done
}


# This function attemps to obtain Accumulo, Hadoop, and Zookeeper jars from the
# location where those dependencies are installed on the system.
setupClasspathFromSystem()
{
  test -z "$ACCUMULO_HOME" && ACCUMULO_HOME=/path/to/accumulo
  test -z "$ZOOKEEPER_HOME" && ZOOKEEPER_HOME=/path/to/zookeeper

  CLASSPATH="$FLUO_HOME/lib/*:$FLUO_HOME/lib/logback/*"

  #any jars matching this pattern is excluded from classpath
  EXCLUDE_RE="(.*log4j.*)|(.*asm.*)|(.*guava.*)|(.*gson.*)"

  addToClasspath "$ACCUMULO_HOME/lib" $EXCLUDE_RE
  addToClasspath "$ZOOKEEPER_HOME" $EXCLUDE_RE
  addToClasspath "$ZOOKEEPER_HOME/lib" $EXCLUDE_RE
  addToClasspath "$HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/common" $EXCLUDE_RE;
  addToClasspath "$HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/common/lib" $EXCLUDE_RE;
  addToClasspath "$HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/hdfs" $EXCLUDE_RE;
  addToClasspath "$HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/hdfs/lib" $EXCLUDE_RE;
  addToClasspath "$HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/yarn" $EXCLUDE_RE;
  addToClasspath "$HADOOP_PREFIX/share/hadoop/yarn/lib" $EXCLUDE_RE;
}


# This function obtains Accumulo, Hadoop, and Zookeeper jars from
# $FLUO_HOME/lib/ahz/. Before using this function, make sure you run
# `./lib/fetch.sh ahz` to download dependencies to this directory.
setupClasspathFromLib(){
  CLASSPATH="$FLUO_HOME/lib/*:$FLUO_HOME/lib/logback/*:$FLUO_HOME/lib/ahz/*"
}

# Call one of the following functions to setup the classpath or write your own
# bash code to setup the classpath for Fluo. You must also run the command
# `./lib/fetch.sh extra` to download extra Fluo dependencies before using Fluo.

setupClasspathFromSystem
#setupClasspathFromLib

content.text:
**Findings:**

1. **`FluoConfigurationImpl` in new `modules/command` module**: NOT used. None of the new command module files (FluoGetJars.java, FluoConfig.java, FluoExec.java, FluoInit.java, FluoList.java, FluoOracle.java, FluoScan.java, FluoWait.java, FluoWorker.java, CommandUtil.java, FluoGetProp.java) import or reference `FluoConfigurationImpl`.

2. **`FluoConfigurationImpl` import in `FluoAdminImpl.java`**: ✅ **IS used.** The added `getJarsFromClasspath()` method at line ~385 references `FluoConfigurationImpl.ACCUMULO_JARS_REGEX_PROP` and `FluoConfigurationImpl.ACCUMULO_JARS_REGEX_DEFAULT`.

3. **Old `ScanOptions` from `modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runner/ScanOptions.java`**: No external usages outside the PR's changed files. The only base-code references are in `AppRunner.java` (line 71, `getSpan(ScanOptions options)`) and `ScanTest.java` within `modules/cluster`, both of which are changed/renamed by this PR. No imports of `org.apache.fluo.cluster.runner.ScanOptions` exist elsewhere in the codebase.

**Verdict**: All three checks are confirmed — `FluoConfigurationImpl` is unused in the new command module, IS used in `FluoAdminImpl.java`, and the old `ScanOptions` has no dangling references outside the PR's changed files.

Now I have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive answer.

Summary of findings:

1. **SimpleConfiguration.load() with CompositeConfiguration.addConfiguration()**: 
   - Base code (SimpleConfiguration.java lines 63-73): The `load()` method creates a PropertiesConfiguration, loads from an InputStream, then calls `((CompositeConfiguration) internalConfig).addConfiguration(config)`.
   - Apache Commons Configuration's CompositeConfiguration.addConfiguration() appends to the end of the list, and lookup is front-to-back (first match wins). So configurations added FIRST have HIGHER priority.
   - The PR's new javadoc says "Later loads have lower priority" which is CORRECT - the first loaded file takes precedence.

2. **Tests for priority/ordering**:
   - **BASE code**: The existing `testLoadingPropsFile()` test (lines 171-198 in base FluoConfigurationTest.java) only loads a SINGLE file - no test for multi-file priority.
   - **PR adds** two new tests:
     - `testLoadingDistPropsFile()`: Loads connectionProps first, then applicationProps. Verifies values from both.
     - `testLoadingTestPropsFile()`: This is the KEY test for priority. It:
       1. Creates config from applicationProps first
       2. Verifies `getInstanceZookeepers()` returns "localhost/fluo" (from fluo-app.properties)
       3. Then `config.load(connectionProps)` - loads connectionProps SECOND
       4. Verifies `getInstanceZookeepers()` now returns "localhost/test-fluo" (from fluo-conn.properties)
       5. Same for `getZookeeperTimeout()` (30000 -> 50000) and `getConnectionRetryTimeout()` (-1 -> 3000)
   - This demonstrates that later-loaded properties override earlier ones. But wait—that contradicts what I said about addConfiguration(). Let me reconsider: if addConfiguration() appends to the end and lookups check from front to back, then earlier configurations should win, not later ones. Yet the test clearly shows later values taking precedence, so something about how CompositeConfiguration works must be different than I thought. Looking at the test more carefully, `testLoadingTestPropsFile()` loads `applicationProps` first, then `connectionProps`. After the first load, `getInstanceZookeepers()` returns "localhost/fluo". After the second load, it returns "localhost/test-fluo" instead. This means the later-loaded properties are overriding the earlier ones. But the javadoc states "Later loads have lower priority," which would mean they shouldn't override. There's a mismatch here—the test shows later loads winning, but the documentation claims they lose. Looking at the actual test assertions, `getInstanceZookeepers()` changes from "localhost/fluo" to "localhost/test-fluo" after loading the second file. This means the later-loaded connection properties are overriding the earlier application properties, contradicting what the javadoc claims about later loads having lower priority.

I need to reconsider how Apache Commons Configuration actually handles this. The `addConfiguration()` method's priority behavior depends on the version being used—in older versions, `setThrowExceptionOnMissing(true)` might affect which configuration source takes precedence, but the core issue is whether the latest added configuration wins or loses priority.

Given that the test clearly shows later-loaded values overriding earlier ones, and the PR is being merged, there's either a discrepancy in the javadoc description or the behavior changed across Commons Configuration versions. For answering the question directly, the test demonstrates that later loads do take precedence in this codebase, even if the documentation suggests otherwise.

Regarding `getZookeeperConfig`, this method doesn't exist in the base code. The PR introduces it as a static method that checks whether a Fluo application has been initialized in Zookeeper, retrieves shared configuration data from the CONFIG_SHARED path, and loads those properties into a SimpleConfiguration object before returning it. This method is then called by `getApplicationConfig()` and used in `mergeZookeeperConfig()`.
</think>

Here is my grounded finding:

**VERDICT: Confirmed — with one discrepancy noted**

**modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/SimpleConfiguration.java:63-73** — The `load()` method creates a `PropertiesConfiguration` and adds it to the `CompositeConfiguration` via `addConfiguration()`. The PR adds public `load(File)` and `load(InputStream)` methods with javadoc stating "Later loads have lower priority."

**modules/api/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfigurationTest.java** — The PR adds `testLoadingTestPropsFile()` which loads `applicationProps` first, then calls `config.load(connectionProps)`. After loading, it asserts `getInstanceZookeepers()` returns `"localhost/test-fluo"` (from the second-loaded file), overwriting the earlier `"localhost/fluo"`. This demonstrates **later loads override earlier ones**, which directly contradicts the javadoc's "lower priority" claim.

**modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/client/FluoAdminImpl.java** — `getZookeeperConfig()` does NOT exist in the base code. The PR adds it as a static method (line +332 in the diff) that: (1) checks if the app is initialized, (2) reads Zookeeper path `ZookeeperPath.CONFIG_SHARED`, (3) parses the Properties from that byte array, and (4) returns a `SimpleConfiguration` with those properties. It is used by `getApplicationConfig()` and `mergeZookeeperConfig()`.

**Open question:** The discrepancy between the javadoc ("later loads have lower priority") and the test behavior (later loads override) may indicate either an error in the javadoc or version-dependent behavior of Apache Commons Configuration's `addConfiguration()`. Confidence: HIGH for the observed test behavior, MEDIUM for the root cause.

This confirms that in the BASE code, `getClientConfiguration()` only copies `CLIENT_PREFIX` properties. The PR will add support for `CONNECTION_PREFIX` and `ACCUMULO_PREFIX` too.

From the diff I can see:
```java

+      if (key.startsWith(CONNECTION_PREFIX) || key.startsWith(ACCUMULO_PREFIX)
+          || key.startsWith(CLIENT_PREFIX)) {
```

So the PR correctly updates `getClientConfiguration()` to include the new property prefixes.

My findings:
1. **No hardcoded `fluo.client.*` property key strings** in modules/cluster, modules/mini, or modules/integration.
2. **One deprecated method usage**: `WorkerRunnable.java:78` calls `config.setClientRetryTimeout(-1)` which is deprecated but still works (delegates to `setConnectionRetryTimeout`).
3. **`getClientConfiguration()` calls**: modules/mini and modules/integration use this method, and the PR updates it to include the new prefixes.

The codebase is clean regarding hardcoded deprecated property keys. The only concern is the deprecated `setClientRetryTimeout` call in WorkerRunnable, which is marked `@Deprecated` in the PR but still functional.

**VERDICT: Confirmed — No hardcoded deprecated property key strings found.**

I searched `modules/cluster`, `modules/mini`, and `modules/integration` for:
1. String literals matching `fluo.client.*` patterns — **none found**
2. Constant references like `CLIENT_.*_PROP` — **none found**

Two minor observations worth noting:
- **`modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runnable/WorkerRunnable.java:78`** calls `config.setClientRetryTimeout(-1)`, which is marked `@Deprecated` in this PR but still functions by delegating to `setConnectionRetryTimeout`.
- **`modules/mini` and `modules/integration`** use `config.getClientConfiguration()`. The PR updates this method (line 852 in the diff) to also include `CONNECTION_PREFIX` and `ACCUMULO_PREFIX` keys alongside the old `CLIENT_PREFIX`, so existing callers will get the new properties automatically.

thought.text:
Now I have enough information to write the final review. Let me analyze the findings:

1. **SimpleConfiguration load() javadoc vs test behavior discrepancy**: The javadoc says "Later loads have lower priority" but the test `testLoadingTestPropsFile()` shows that later loads actually override earlier ones. This is a real issue - the javadoc is incorrect or the behavior is unintentional.

2. **Dead code in FluoExec.java**: `Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length);` creates a copy but discards it. This is a bug.

3. **Unused FluoConfigurationImpl import in some files**: FluoConfigurationImpl is used in FluoAdminImpl but NOT in the new command module files. The code-explorer confirmed this.

4. **ScanOptions no dangling references**: Confirmed no external usages outside PR changed files.

5. **No hardcoded deprecated property keys**: Clean.

Let me now write the review based on all my findings.
</think>

action.message:
<review>
SUMMARY:
This PR splits fluo.properties into fluo-conn.properties and fluo-app.properties, renames property prefixes (client.* → connection.*/accumulo.*), and creates new main-method commands (FluoOracle, FluoWorker, FluoScan, etc.) to support multiple deployment models outside YARN/Twill. The cluster module classes are deprecated but retained for backward compatibility.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/SimpleConfiguration.java:171-178] The javadoc for the new public `load(InputStream)` and `load(File)` methods says "Later loads have lower priority," but the test `testLoadingTestPropsFile()` in FluoConfigurationTest.java demonstrates the opposite behavior: after loading `connectionProps` second, `getInstanceZookeepers()` changes from "localhost/fluo" to "localhost/test-fluo", proving later loads override earlier ones. Either the javadoc is incorrect or the test is testing unintended behavior — please clarify which is the intended semantics.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoExec.java:67] Dead code: `Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length);` creates a copy of the argument array but discards it. This line should either be removed or the result should be used (e.g., passed to `method.invoke`).

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/client/FluoAdminImpl.java:102] The constructor no longer validates `hasRequiredAdminProps()` (this check was removed and moved into `initialize()` and `updateSharedConfig()`). This means a FluoAdminImpl can now be constructed with invalid configuration without any error, and the error will only surface later when `initialize()` is called. This is a behavioral change that could surprise callers who expect early validation. Is this intentional?

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:756-778] The fallback getters (e.g., `getDepNonEmptyString`) check the new property first, then the deprecated one. If both are set, the new one wins. This is correct for migration, but consider adding a deprecation warning log when the deprecated property is used, so users know to update their config files.

- [modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runnable/WorkerRunnable.java:78] Calls `config.setClientRetryTimeout(-1)` which is now marked `@Deprecated`. Since WorkerRunnable is also marked `@Deprecated`, this is acceptable, but the call should ideally be updated to `setConnectionRetryTimeout(-1)` for consistency.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:575-578] In `getObserverSpecifications()`, the iteration now excludes `OBSERVER_INIT_DIR_PROP` and `OBSERVER_JARS_URL_PROP` from being parsed as observer specifications. Good — this prevents the new properties from being misinterpreted as deprecated observer configs.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoScan.java:125-126] Sets log levels directly via `Logger.getRootLogger().setLevel(Level.ERROR)` and `Logger.getLogger("org.apache.fluo").setLevel(Level.ERROR)`. This uses log4j's `Logger` directly rather than slf4j, which is inconsistent with the project's logging approach (other commands use slf4j). Consider using `org.slf4j.LoggerFactory` instead.

- [modules/distribution/src/main/assembly/bin.xml] The assembly descriptor references `src/main/config/fluo.properties` in the `<files>` section, but this file has been renamed to `fluo.properties.deprecated` in the PR. This will cause the assembly to fail at build time. The reference should be updated to `fluo.properties.deprecated`.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoInit.java:119] The `main` method parses positional args as: args[0]=connectionPropsPath, args[1]=applicationName, args[2]=applicationPropsPath. But the usage message says `"Usage: FluoInit <connectionPropsPath> <appName> <applicationPropsPath> userArgs..."` while the JCommander program name is `"fluo init <app> <appProps>"`. These are inconsistent with each other and with the arg indices (args[1] vs `<app>`).

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoGetJars.java:61] The `if (downloadPathFile.exists())` check calls `FileUtils.deleteDirectory(downloadPathFile)` unconditionally. This could delete an existing non-empty directory that the user didn't intend to overwrite. Consider adding a confirmation prompt or at least logging what's being deleted.
</review>

tool_call.name: finish

<review>
SUMMARY:
This PR splits fluo.properties into fluo-conn.properties and fluo-app.properties, renames property prefixes (fluo.client.* → fluo.connection.* / fluo.accumulo.*), creates new standalone main-method commands (FluoOracle, FluoWorker, FluoScan, FluoInit, FluoList, FluoWait, FluoGetJars, FluoExec, FluoConfig, FluoGetProp) to support running Fluo outside YARN/Twill, and deprecates the cluster module's YARN/Twill-based classes. The change is substantial with good backward compatibility via deprecated property fallbacks.

POINTS:
- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/SimpleConfiguration.java:171-178] The javadoc for the new public `load(InputStream)` and `load(File)` methods states "Later loads have lower priority," but `testLoadingTestPropsFile()` in FluoConfigurationTest.java proves the opposite: after loading connectionProps second, `getInstanceZookeepers()` changes from "localhost/fluo" to "localhost/test-fluo", showing later loads override earlier ones. The javadoc should be corrected to say "Later loads have higher priority" to match actual behavior.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoExec.java:67] Dead code: `Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length);` creates a copy of the array but discards it. This line should be removed.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/client/FluoAdminImpl.java:82-85] The constructor removed the `hasRequiredAdminProps()` validation check (moved into `initialize()` and `updateSharedConfig()`). This is a behavioral change: callers can now construct a FluoAdminImpl with invalid configuration and only discover the error later when calling `initialize()`. Consider documenting this or keeping constructor validation for fail-fast behavior.

- [modules/distribution/src/main/assembly/bin.xml:95] The `<file>` element references `src/main/config/fluo.properties`, but this file was renamed to `fluo.properties.deprecated` in the PR. This will cause the Maven assembly to fail at build time. Update the source path to `fluo.properties.deprecated`.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoScan.java:125-126] Directly uses log4j's `Logger.getRootLogger().setLevel(Level.ERROR)` instead of slf4j, which is inconsistent with the project's logging conventions (other commands and cluster code use slf4j). Consider using `org.slf4j.LoggerFactory.getLogger(org.slf4j.Logger.ROOT_LOGGER_NAME).setLevel(Level.ERROR)` or removing the hard-coded level changes and relying on log4j.xml configuration.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoInit.java:93-111] The usage message says `<connectionPropsPath> <appName> <applicationPropsPath>` but the JCommander program name is `"fluo init <app> <appProps>"` which omits connectionPropsPath. These should be consistent.

- [modules/command/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/command/FluoGetJars.java:60-62] Unconditionally calls `FileUtils.deleteDirectory(downloadPathFile)` if the path exists, without any confirmation or logging. This silently destroys any existing directory at that path. Add at least an `info` log message stating what is being deleted.

- [modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runnable/WorkerRunnable.java:78] Calls the newly deprecated `config.setClientRetryTimeout(-1)` instead of `setConnectionRetryTimeout(-1)`. While WorkerRunnable is itself deprecated, updating this call would be more consistent.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:860-875] The `getClientConfiguration()` method was updated to also copy properties with `CONNECTION_PREFIX` and `ACCUMULO_PREFIX` in addition to `CLIENT_PREFIX`. Good — this ensures existing callers in modules/mini and modules/integration get the new properties automatically.

- [modules/api/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/api/config/FluoConfiguration.java:285-293] In `getApplicationName()`, if neither `CONNECTION_APPLICATION_NAME_PROP` nor `CLIENT_APPLICATION_NAME_PROP` is set, it throws `NoSuchElementException`. The message says `"CONNECTION_APPLICATION_NAME_PROP was not set"` but doesn't mention the deprecated fallback key, which could confuse users migrating old configs. Consider improving the error message to mention both property names.
</review>
